stem: A clinical study study measures the diastolic blood pressure (mmHg) for a sample of 30 patients. The sample mean is 106.8 mmHg with a sample standard deviation of 30.7 mmHg. However, 3 of the 30 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 27 observations have a mean of 102.7 mmHg and standard deviation of 20.6 mmHg. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 106.8, 95% CI = [95.3, 118.3]. Without outliers: mean = 102.7, 95% CI = [94.8, 110.6]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [94.8, 118.3]. Point estimate: 104.8 mmHg.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [94.8, 118.3].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
